_comment: Pterodactyl Egg — Ranger Aide Memoire MkDocs wiki. Polls a GitHub content repo, renders MkDocs Material, serves the built site on the allocated port.
name: Ranger Aide Memoire Wiki
author: admin@5ranger.com
description: MkDocs Material wiki for the 5 RANGER unit, fed from a GitHub content repo. entry.py polls the repo every SYNC_INTERVAL seconds; on a new commit it downloads the tarball, renders mkdocs.yml from manifest.yaml, runs `python -m mkdocs build`, and serves the result via stdlib http.server. Single foreground process, one port. Bring-up: ~2 min on first install (apt + pip + initial build), <10 s on subsequent restarts (deps cached in .pydeps/, build skipped on unchanged SHA).

installation.script:
#!/bin/bash
# ── Ranger Aide Memoire Wiki install script ───────────────────────────────
# Runs once on server creation or Reinstall. Clones the code repo and exits.
# Python deps are bootstrapped by entry.py on first boot (into .pydeps/ on
# the persistent volume) so a dep bump survives container image rebuilds
# without requiring a Reinstall.

set -e

echo 'Installing host packages (git only — the rest is pure-Python)…'
apt-get update -y -q
apt-get install -y -q git curl

echo 'Cloning code repository…'
git clone https://github.com/Gibsx/5-RANGER-MkDocs /mnt/server
cd /mnt/server

chown -R container:container /mnt/server

echo ''
echo '────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────'
echo 'Install complete. Set REPO + TOKEN on the Startup tab and Start.'
echo 'First boot: ~60–120s to bootstrap pip deps + run initial sync.'
echo 'Subsequent boots: <10s (deps cached in .pydeps/ on the volume).'
echo 'Watch console for "Sync loop running every Ns. Press Ctrl-C to exit."'
echo '────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────'

variables.name: Content Repo
variables.description: owner/name of the content repository containing manifest.yaml and the section markdown. Example: Gibsx/RANGER-AIDE-MEMOIRE. Same repo Robinson syncs from — both consumers stay in lockstep because both poll the same SHA.

variables.name: Content Branch
variables.description: Branch of the content repo to track. Almost always 'main'.

variables.name: GitHub Token
variables.description: Fine-grained GitHub Personal Access Token with Contents: Read on the content repo. github.com/settings/personal-access-tokens/new — fine-grained, single-repo, Contents:Read only. Required even for public repos because GitHub's anonymous quota is 60/hr; authenticated is 5000/hr.

variables.name: Sync Interval Seconds
variables.description: How often entry.py polls the content repo for a new commit SHA. 60 s is the default — translates to 60 GitHub API calls per hour, well under the 5000/hr authenticated quota. Polls on unchanged SHA are cheap (one HEAD-equivalent call, no build).

variables.name: Site Name
variables.description: MkDocs site_name — appears in the wiki header and browser tab title.

variables.name: Site Description
variables.description: MkDocs site_description — used in HTML meta tags and social-share previews.
variables.default_value: 5th Battalion, Ranger Regiment — doctrine, SOPs, and field craft.
